text: The surgeon has removed adhesions from the anterior abdominal wall, uterus, and intestines on the side wall. The uterus appears to be in good condition, and a small lymph node is visible but not causing symptoms.

The surgeon observes the water tank attached to the front abdominal wall, the womb, and the uterus. The tank is carefully dissected and cut from all directions to achieve complete liberation from these structures.

The surgeon identifies and cuts film adhesions from distant areas, with a visible mass located nearby. The operation progresses to ensure complete liberation of the adhesions. Blood vessels are cleaned and confirmed to be closed, followed by placing and securing a plastic bag within the surgical field.
